sections.paragraph:
m. plur. תֻּפִּים (from the root תָּפַף)-

(1) a drum, timbrel (Arab. دُفُّ, whence the Spanish adduffa), beaten in the East by women when dancing; it is made with a wooden circle, covered with membrane and furnished with brass bells, Exo 15:20 Jud. 11:34 Jer 31:4 (compare Psa 68:26). Compare Niebuhr’s Travels, vol i. p. 181.

(2) Eze 28:13, the bezel or hollow in which a gem is set; compare נֶקֶב.
